title: task-success bench scenario
description: Formalizes the shape harness/tasks/task-scenarios.json entries already have, and harness/task_runner.py already reads. This is documentation-after-the-fact of an existing implicit shape, derived by reading task-scenarios.json's 18 entries and task_runner.py's read_scenarios()/run_one_scenario(); it does not add or invent any field. Validated by harness/tasks/validate_schemas.py (hand-rolled, stdlib-only - no jsonschema dependency in this repo).

id.description: Stable scenario id, unique within task-scenarios.json - used as the storage key for the seeded script (task_runner.py's seed_script()/run_seeded_script()) and as the lookup key into a Phase A authored-*.json's goals map.

tier.description: fixture = this repo's self-contained local mini-site (harness/corpus/tasks/), realsite = a live external site (currently Wikipedia only). Selected by task_runner.py's --tier flag and used by nav_origin_allowed() to pick the nav-confirm origin allowlist.

goal.description: Plain-English goal text sent to the authoring model in Phase A (harness/author_tasks.py). Not read at all by Phase B (task_runner.py) - Phase B only ever seeds the already-authored script body.

start_page.description: Path relative to harness/corpus/, joined onto http://127.0.0.1:<PORT_A>/ as the page task_runner.py navigates to before seeding and running the script (run_one_scenario()'s start_url).

run_args.description: Positional args appended to the seeded script's `run <name> [args...]` invocation (run_seeded_script()). Empty in every current scenario; quoted at invocation time if an arg contains a space.

success.description: Ordered list of success checks, ALL of which must pass (evaluate_checks()/run_one_scenario()'s checks_ok) for a completed/paused run to be scored a success. See classify() in task_runner.py for the full state machine these feed into.

expect_pause.description: True for a goal whose script is meant to park at a `pause` step rather than run to completion (classify(): a paused run without expect_pause is bucket pause_unexpected; a completed run WITH expect_pause is bucket wrong_plan).

timeout_s.description: Per-scenario wall-clock budget for run_seeded_script()/watch_run() before the row is scored bucket timeout.

min_steps_executed.description: OPTIONAL. When present, an otherwise-passing row whose observed step count (preferring the product's own lastRunVerdict.stepsTotal when available, falling back to the polling step_dispatched counter) is below this floor is demoted to success=false, bucket=wrong_plan - closes the path-dependent-goal gameability FIX 2 documents in task_runner.py and harness/README.md's task-success section. Only set on task-scenarios.json's shop-open-item-back-to-products today.

type.description: Matches one of evaluate_checks()'s three implemented check functions in task_runner.py (check_url_contains/check_text_visible/check_field_value). Any other value is a recognized-but-unimplemented check at scoring time (evaluate_checks()'s else branch scores it ok=false with an 'unknown check type' error) - this schema restricts task-scenarios.json authoring to the three that are actually implemented.

value.description: url_contains: a substring page.url must contain. text_visible: a substring the check looks for via page.get_by_text() first, page.content() as fallback. field_value: the exact expected value of the field named by `selector`.

selector.description: REQUIRED when type is field_value (a CSS selector passed to page.locator(selector).input_value()); absent for url_contains/text_visible.
